titleStmt.title: Letter from William R. Fetherstonhaugh to Sir Matthew Nathan, 17 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: William R. Fetherstonhaugh

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from William Fetherstonhaugh to Sir Matthew Nathan enclosing a receipt for the six-monthly allowance paid to the Under Sheriff by the High Sheriff.William Fetherstonhaugh (b. 1871) was a land agent. The Featherstonhaugh family held estates in the midlands of Ireland, especially county Westmeath, since the seventeenth century. Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/5596/164/9

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
From W. R. FETHERSTONHAUGH, Sheriff's Office, MULLINGAR 17th Decb 1915. To The Under Secretary Dublin Castle Sir, In reply to your letter of
16th inst. I beg to enclose
your receipt to High Sherriff
for second moiety of
Salary allowed him for
Under Sherriff â I am
Yr obt servant W.R. Fetherstonhaugh Payment
ordered
RP
21/12
